expectations.text: To prevent wasted outreach to a stale contact, the output flags David Hargreaves for potential role change (no LinkedIn updates in >12 months) rather than assuming he is still Head of Ops at Brightpath Consulting -- a wrong-title email tanks response rates
expectations.evidence: Section 1.2 'Title Change Flag' is marked 'STATUS: LIKELY ROLE CHANGE -- VERIFICATION REQUIRED.' It explains: '14 months with no profile updates is a signal that either (a) the title is now stale or (b) David has moved roles internally.' The risk is explicitly stated: 'Addressing David by a stale title in outreach damages credibility and tanks response rates. For a Tier 1 account, this is a high-impact error.' The output flags David for probable promotion to VP Revenue Operations and does not assume the old title is current.

expectations.text: To protect revenue on an active deal, the output does NOT overwrite the manually-entered direct phone number -- it explicitly flags it as a human-entered field and defers to Jess Moreno as record owner before any change, since reps trust CRM data they entered themselves
expectations.evidence: The CRITICAL GUARDRAILS table states: 'Direct phone (David Hargreaves) | PROTECTED -- NOT OVERWRITTEN | Manually entered by Jess Moreno. Human-entered field flagged as owner-verified. No automated overwrite applied.' The mandatory field status table confirms: 'PROTECTED -- No change applied | Owner-entered field | LOCKED -- Human-entered, do not overwrite.'

expectations.text: To protect the active nurture sequence from breaking, the output does NOT remove David Hargreaves from the sequence due to any email change -- instead it specifies pausing the sequence and alerting Jess Moreno to verify new contact details first, preventing a live prospect from going dark
expectations.evidence: Section 1.3 'Active Nurture Sequence Protection' provides a detailed 6-step workflow: '1. PAUSE the nurture sequence for David Hargreaves, 2. Alert Jess Moreno with the old and new email addresses, 3. Jess verifies the correct email directly, 4. Update the email field in CRM, 5. RESUME the sequence only after Jess confirms the new email is valid, 6. Verify that all personalization tokens in remaining sequence steps still resolve correctly.' It explicitly states: 'Do NOT auto-resume. Jess must approve.'

expectations.text: To enable accurate segmentation and lead routing after the Series B, account-level enrichment addresses Brightpath Consulting headcount with a flag if the change exceeds 20%, plus the Series B funding captured as a news event -- these fields drive tier classification and ICP scoring
expectations.evidence: Section 2.3 'Headcount Change Flag' states: 'STATUS: LIKELY >20% HEADCOUNT INCREASE -- VERIFICATION REQUIRED. Post-Series B companies almost universally scale headcount aggressively. A >20% headcount change triggers: Tier classification review, ICP scoring recalculation, Lead routing check.' Section 2.2 'Series B Funding Event' captures the funding as a news event with specific fields to populate: 'Event Type: Funding Round, Event Detail: Series B Close, Event Date: (confirm exact date), Source URL: (link to press release or Crunchbase entry).'

expectations.text: To ensure the QBR uses reliable data, the output includes a DATA QUALITY SCORE as a percentage of enriched records now complete, plus a RECORDS WITH MISSING MANDATORY FIELDS section listing any fields still unfilled -- giving the rep a clear picture of what is verified vs. what still needs manual work
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'DATA QUALITY SCORE' provides percentage scores: 'Contact Data Quality Score: 50% verified (3 of 6 mandatory fields confirmed),' 'Account Data Quality Score: 0% fully verified (0 of 5 mandatory fields confirmed post-enrichment),' 'Overall Enrichment Quality Score: 27% verified (3 of 11 total mandatory fields fully confirmed).' Section 4 'RECORDS WITH MISSING OR UNVERIFIED MANDATORY FIELDS' lists specific fields with issues and owner actions required for both the contact (Current Title, Work Email, LinkedIn URL) and account (Legal Name, Revenue Estimate, Headcount, Headquarters, Website).

expectations.text: To drive accurate pipeline forecasting, the output covers all six contact-level mandatory fields (full name, current title, current company, work email, direct phone, LinkedIn URL) and all five account-level mandatory fields (legal name, revenue estimate, headcount, headquarters, website) -- incomplete records create routing errors and missed handoffs
expectations.evidence: Section 1.1 covers all six contact-level mandatory fields in the table: Full Name, Current Title, Current Company, Work Email, Direct Phone, LinkedIn URL. Section 2.1 covers all five account-level mandatory fields: Legal Name, Revenue Estimate, Headcount, Headquarters, Website. Each field has previous value, enriched value, source, and action taken columns.
